title: Hypogynium heteroclitum
intro: Hypogynium heteroclitum, commonly known as the "Pau'ahana", is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The health benefits of Pau'ahana include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Hawaiian medicine, Pau'ahana is often prepared as a tea or a decoction, and is also used in topical preparations, such as salves and liniments, to promote wound healing and reduce pain.
taxonomy: Hypogynium heteroclitum is classified as a species of liverwort, a non-vascular land plant. It belongs to the genus Hypogynium and family Calypogeiaceae. The classification of liverworts is still evolving, but current classifications place Hypogynium heteroclitum within the division Marchantiophyta. This division includes over 9,000 species of liverworts, which are found on every continent except Antarctica. The classification of Hypogynium heteroclitum is subject to revision as new research emerges.
